Panel complete. 5 rounds, 50 bus entries, 4 final verdicts (3 ACCEPT-with-caveats, 1 REJECT-as-drafted-ACCEPT-with-fixes — substantively same converged fix list). Consensus at panel/v0.7.0-rename-review/consensus.md enumerates 7 ship-blocker clusters (SB-A..SB-G) with named resolutions and unanimous verdicts on §9 Q1-Q5. Panel recommends holding bead moat-02h (plan) until rename spec is edited per SB-A..SB-G.

Dispositions applied to panel/v0.7.0-rename-review/consensus.md §Dispositions. Key override: single-adopter reality. SB-A IGNORE, SB-C drop fallback entirely, SB-B/D/E/F/G applied (simplified where fallback machinery is no longer needed). Q1-Q5 resolved. Most should-fix items N/A under single-adopter scope. moat-hqm has a 10-item edit list and explicit out-of-scope list.

Panel dispositions applied to .develop/v0.7.0-rename-spec.md. §4 rewritten as hard-cut (no fallback preservation); §5 adds single-commit atomicity requirement for both publisher and registry migrations; Phase 1 scope expanded in §6 and success criteria in §7 include .moat UCD exclusion, snippet update, .moat/ reservation section, SHOULD-level actionable errors, and simplified grep predicates (returns 0); §8 no-dual-read parenthetical removed (fallback dropped); §9 rewritten as Resolutions (Q1-Q5 baked in, panel-deferred items explicitly out of scope); §10 traceability updated. Status: ready for plan bead moat-02h.

Wrote .develop/v0.7.0-rename-plan.md with per-file search/replace tables for phases 1–5, grep validation predicates per phase, rollback notes. Surfaced 2 spec gaps for parity/sanity: F-1 specs/moat-verify.md missing from Phase 1 scope (has 2 hits; grep success criterion implicitly covers it); F-2 §5 publisher step 3 paths-trigger edit is a no-op for reference-workflow users because neither live nor reference workflow has a paths: allow-list — only paths-ignore. Plan reconciles the stale bead description (mentioned preserving OIDC fallback) with the post-disposition scope (drop fallback entirely) per single-adopter reality.

Parity check complete. Report at .develop/v0.7.0-parity-report.md. Found 195 hits; 79 covered-explicitly, 89 covered-by-phase-scope, 21 intentionally-excluded, 6 MISSED (all in website/src/content/docs/spec/core.md). Verdict FAIL at check time. Amendment applied to plan: core.md added to Phase 4 in-scope files and §4a step 1 enumerates 4 edits for it; Phase 4 grep validation extended. Plan re-verifies clean after amendment.

Sanity check complete. Report at .develop/v0.7.0-sanity-report.md. 5 PASS, 1 FAIL (website/src/content/docs/spec/core.md missing from Phase 4 — same finding as parity), 1 WARN (specs/publisher-action.md:49 has two moat.yml occurrences on one line; needs replace_all or disambiguating context). Plan amendments applied: Phase 1a risk note for replace_all; Phase 4 includes core.md; CHANGELOG phrasing softened from 'No consumers on pre-v0.7.0 existed' to 'No backwards compatibility is provided for pre-v0.7.0 attestations.' OIDC fallback DELETION, paths: triggers, YAML validity, CHANGELOG rule compliance, Phase 3 atomicity all PASS. Line numbers in phases 1–3 verified against 6bc986f.

Approved by Holden 2026-04-24 after F-1 (specs/moat-verify.md added to Phase 1 scope in rename spec §6 and §7) and F-2 (§5 publisher step 3 rewritten as explicit conditional with reference-workflow escape hatch; atomicity paragraph softened) were resolved upstream in the rename spec. Plan amendments from parity/sanity (add core.md to Phase 4, replace_all note for specs/publisher-action.md:49, CHANGELOG phrasing softening) also applied. Plan, parity report, and sanity report all current as of this approval.

Phase 1 impl complete. Edited 4 spec files: specs/moat-verify.md (2 edits), moat-spec.md (4 edits), specs/registry-action.md (16 edits + fallback-clause deletion), specs/publisher-action.md (12 edits + 2 additions: .moat/ reservation section + SHOULD-level error messages). Validation greps pass: 0 legacy moat.yml, 0 legacy registry.yml, 0 fall back, 29 .moat/ references across 3 files.

Phase 1 validation PASS. Report at .develop/v0.7.0-phase1-validate.md — all 9 checks pass: uniform rename, fallback deletion, .moat/ reservation section, SHOULD-level error messages, Undiscovered Content exclusion, MUST/SHOULD preservation, no website/reference/.github drift, schema modality preserved.

Phase 2 impl complete. git mv reference/moat.yml → reference/moat-publisher.yml (history preserved). 4 edits in reference/moat-publisher.yml (tier-2 path, undiscovered-content snippet string, publisher_workflow_ref format comment + default). 11 edits in reference/moat-registry.yml (paths: trigger, config_path, error msg, docstrings, publisher fallback defaults). Both YAML files parse clean. Grep validation: zero legacy moat.yml/registry.yml outside .moat/ prefix.

Phase 2 validation PASS. Report at .develop/v0.7.0-phase2-validate.md — all 12 checks pass: git mv rename staged (R100 similarity, history will preserve), 4+11 edits match plan §2a/§2b tables exactly, zero legacy moat.yml/registry.yml strings, YAML valid, no Phase 3/Phase 1 drift. Observation: commit Phase 2 with rename+edits in a single commit so git log --follow preserves history.

Phase 3 impl complete in single atomic commit f636e58. git mv .github/workflows/moat.yml → .github/workflows/moat-publisher.yml (R99, history preserved); git mv registry.yml → .moat/registry.yml (R100, history preserved). 3 edits to moat-publisher.yml + 11 edits to moat-registry.yml. .moat/registry.yml content needed no edits (header references unrenamed registry workflow file). YAML validation passes for all 3 live files. Old files (.github/workflows/moat.yml, repo-root registry.yml) absent.

Phase 3 validation PASS. All 13 checks: single atomic commit f636e58 (R99 + R100), history preserved via git log --follow for both renames, dead-window closed (paths: trigger and config file both at new path in same commit), YAML valid across 3 live files, zero scope leakage. Report at .develop/v0.7.0-phase3-validate.md.

Phase 4 impl complete. 14 files edited, ~100 hits resolved. All grep validations return 0 stale refs (except 4 legitimate migration-prose hits in publisher-action.md §Actionable Error Messages, which mirror the canonical spec). Website build exits 0 in 2.99s. Subagent also repaired stale website mirror at website/src/content/docs/spec/publisher-action.md by adding the Undiscovered Content Detection, .moat/ Directory Reservation, and Actionable Error Messages sections from canonical specs/publisher-action.md.

Phase 4 validate PASS. All 6 grep checks clean (the 4 surviving moat.yml refs are the authorized migration-prose in publisher-action.md §Actionable Error Messages). Website build exits 0 in 2.99s. Canonical ↔ mirror consistency confirmed for Phase 4 rename scope. Scope: only the 14 target files were touched; specs/, moat-spec.md, reference/, .github/workflows/, CHANGELOG all untouched. Pre-existing website mirror drift (unrelated to rename) captured as separate follow-up bead. Report at .develop/v0.7.0-phase4-validate.md.

Phase 5 impl complete. CHANGELOG Unreleased has opening paragraph, Migration publishers section, Migration registry operators section per plan 5a. Conforms to changelog rules. OpenWolf: anatomy.md cleaned, cerebrum.md Decision Log entry added 2026-04-24, memory.md session lines appended.

Phase 5 validate PASS. CHANGELOG Unreleased conforms to all 4 prohibitions and all 3 content requirements in changelog rules. Zero hits for panel/persona/finding-ID patterns. Migration steps verified factually — reference/moat-publisher.yml uses paths-ignore per step 3 qualifier, reference/moat-registry.yml has paths .moat/registry.yml trigger per registry step 2. Anatomy matches filesystem; cerebrum has dated substantive Decision Log entry explaining both the what and the why. Memory has 5 clean session lines. Only expected files in git diff. Anomalies: Migration subsections are new pattern for MOAT (not blocker). Report at .develop/v0.7.0-phase5-validate.md.

Final full-repo sweep PASS across all 10 checks. Zero unexpected stale refs (only migration prose in CHANGELOG + Actionable Error Messages in publisher-action.md, both intentional). Zero fall-back clauses in specs or website mirrors. .moat/ reservation present normatively. YAML parses clean for all 7 workflow/reference/config files. Website build exit 0 with 13 pages, no warnings. git log --follow traces rename history back through R99/R100 detection. CHANGELOG has exactly one Unreleased section with both migration subsections. VERSION correctly still at 0.6.1 (v0.7.0 bump is separate follow-up per plan 8). Report at .develop/v0.7.0-final-report.md.

Release chain approved and pushed. 5 commits shipped to origin main: 3e05e86 specs+reference rename, f636e58 atomic CI rename, ee22c77 docs+website, 522c715 CHANGELOG migration sections, 31239e8 release v0.7.0. VERSION bumped 0.6.1 to 0.7.0 with scripts/bump-version.py propagating to moat-spec.md header, README, website core spec page, spec-status page. CHANGELOG Unreleased rolled over to 0.7.0 2026-04-24 Draft. Remaining RELEASING.md steps 4-6 (git tag v0.7.0, push tags, GitHub Release) pending separate follow-up.
Applied Changes 1-3 to reference/moat-registry.yml (workflow_run trigger + job guard + comment) and added SHOULD language to specs/registry-action.md line 26. YAML parses cleanly with 4 triggers.

Mirrors re-synced by subagent: core.md 894->1121 lines, registry-action.md 185->220 lines. Added: Crawl Optimization section, Trust State Error Vocabulary, Version Transition section, signing_profile v2 with repo-ID pinning, workflow_run SHOULD, Security Considerations. Link rewrites applied per mirror convention.

Sub-spec versions bumped: publisher-action 0.1.0->0.2.0, registry-action 0.1.0->0.2.0, moat-verify stays 0.1.0. Mirrors updated to match. spec-status.md table updated. Reference implementation version strings bumped to v0.2.0 in reference/moat-*.yml. Live .github/workflows/moat-*.yml hard-synced from reference/ per v0.7.1 dogfood scope.
v0.7.1 shipped. Commit b32a3f8, tag v0.7.1, prerelease published at github.com/OpenScribbler/moat/releases/tag/v0.7.1
